title: Auxiliary Laps-To-Stop Target Instead Of Pure Binary Training
summary: A recent F1 pit-stop paper reports a cascaded multi-task design where next-lap classification is complemented by predicting remaining laps to the stop.
rationale: A recent F1 pit-stop paper reports a cascaded multi-task design where next-lap classification is complemented by predicting remaining laps to the stop. That is a more informative target than a lone `PitNextLap` bit and can regularize ambiguous pre-stop rows.
implementation_hint: From train only, compute causal `laps_until_next_pit` within each driver-race sequence. Train either: `1)` a separate regressor/ordinal model for capped distance bins like `0,1,2,3,4+`, or `2)` a hazard-style model for the next 1-3 laps. Feed its OOF prediction into the main classifier or blend the outputs. Treat race-end censoring explicitly.
expected_effect: Smoother supervision around the stop window, less label noise, and sharper probabilities on the few laps before a true stop.
risk: Censoring at race end and wet-race behavior can distort the auxiliary label if not handled separately.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
